standard_description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, possessive).
subject: Language

khan_exercises.title: Choosing between subject and object pronouns
khan_exercises.description: Identify the best form for the personal pronoun.
topic_path: Choosing between subject and object pronouns

items.question_content:
**Choose the best form for the personal pronoun: subjective, objective, or possessive!**

>Irene put the sandwiches in  \_\_\_\_\_\_  backpack.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: In this instance, our pronoun isn't a subject or object. Instead, it's showing possession.
We should use the possessive adjective **"her".**

items.question_content:
**Choose the best form for the personal pronoun: subjective, objective, or possessive!**

>Walter will just have to do what's best for  \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The **subject** is the part of a sentence that _does stuff_.  

* **He** walks to school.

The **object** is the part of the sentence that _has stuff done to it_:

* The dog licked **him**.

Subject pronouns include *I, we, he, she, you, they, who* and *it*.

Object pronouns include *me, us, him, her, you, them, whom* and *it.*
In this sentence, our underlined pronoun isn't the subject of any verb, so we should use the object pronoun **"him"**.

Subject pronouns include *I, we, he, she, you, they, who* and *it*.

Object pronouns include *me, us, him, her, you, them, whom* and *it.*
In this sentence, our underlined pronoun isn't the subject of any verb, so we should use the object pronoun **"me"**.

In this sentence, the underlined pronoun is the subject of the verb "decided", so we should use the subject pronoun **"I"**.

**Choose the best form for the personal pronoun: subjective, objective, or possessive!**

>It’s too bad  \_\_\_\_\_\_  can’t come to the concert.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The **subject** is the part of a sentence that _does stuff_.  

* **He** walks to school.

The **object** is the part of the sentence that _has stuff done to it_:

* The dog licked **him**.

Subject pronouns include *I, we, he, she, you, they, who* and *it*.

Object pronouns include *me, us, him, her, you, them, whom* and *it.*
For the second person, "you" is both the subject and the object pronoun. Since we're not showing possession, we should use the pronoun **"you"**.

The **object** is the part of the sentence that _has stuff done to it_:

In this sentence, the underlined pronoun is the subject of the verb "are planning", so we should use the subject pronoun **"they"**.

items.question_content:
**Choose the best form for the personal pronoun: subjective, objective, or possessive!**

>Shirley took her car to the gas station and gave  \_\_\_\_\_\_  a car wash.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The **subject** is the part of a sentence that _does stuff_.  

* **He** walks to school.

The **object** is the part of the sentence that _has stuff done to it_:

* The dog licked **him**.

Subject pronouns include *I, we, he, she, you, they, who* and *it*.

Object pronouns include *me, us, him, her, you, them, whom* and *it.*
When referring to a singular noun without gender, "it" is both the subject and the object pronoun. Since we're not showing possession, we should use the pronoun **"it"**.

>The students in Ms. Echolls’ class cheered;  \_\_\_\_\_\_  were going to the dinosaur museum!

hints:

Subject pronouns include *I, we, he, she, you, they, who* and *it*.

Object pronouns include *me, us, him, her, you, them, whom* and *it.*
In this sentence, the underlined pronoun is the subject of the verb "were going", so we should use the subject pronoun **"they"**.

khan_exercises.title: Pronoun person
khan_exercises.description: Ask yourself: "Who is this sentence talking about?"  Then choose the pronoun that makes the most sense.

items.question_content:
**Which pronoun makes the most sense in this sentence?**

>When you go on a trip,  \_\_\_\_\_\_ should pack plenty of layers.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronoun person distinguishes among "me/us" (first person), "you" (second person), and "he, she, one, it, they, etc." (third person).

The pronoun we select should agree in person with two things: 1) the noun the pronoun describes, and 2) other pronouns used in the sentence.
This sentence uses the second person pronoun "you" elsewhere in the sentence. So, we should use the pronoun **"you"** again to match.

items.question_content:
**Which pronoun makes the most sense in this sentence?**

>I couldn’t sleep because  \_\_\_\_\_\_ kept convincing myself there was something outside.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronoun person distinguishes among "me/us" (first person), "you" (second person), and "he, she, one, it, they, etc." (third person).

The pronoun we select should agree in person with two things: 1) the noun the pronoun describes, and 2) other pronouns used in the sentence.
This sentence uses the first person pronoun "I" elsewhere in the sentence. So, we should use the pronoun **"I"** to match.

khan_exercises.title: Pronoun number
khan_exercises.description: Is the pronoun in this sentence singular or plural?  Use context to choose the pronoun that makes the most sense.

items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>Have you seen Zoila and Betsey? I can’t find  \_\_\_\_\_\_ anywhere.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence refers to two people, Zoila and Betsey, so we should use the *plural* pronoun **them**.

items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

> Making a snow angel is one of Margarita’s favorite pastimes, even though  \_\_\_\_\_\_ makes her cold.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This pronoun refers to the act of "making a snow angel" so we should use the *singular* pronoun **it**.

items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>Natalie balanced four plates on  \_\_\_\_\_\_ head.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence refers to "Natalie" so we should use the *singular* possessive adjective **her**.

items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>While  \_\_\_\_\_\_ on a business trip, I ate the rest of Muneera’s carrots without her permission.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This pronoun refers to "Maneera" so we should use the *singular* possessive adjective **her**.

items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>The mouse peeked  \_\_\_\_\_\_ head out of the cookies in the cookie jar.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This pronoun refers to "mouse" so we should use the *singular* possessive adjective **its**.

items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>As the first student from this school to win the regional spelling bee, you should be very proud of  \_\_\_\_\_\_!

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This pronoun refers to "you" so we should use the *singular* reflexive pronoun **yourself**.

items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>Genet considered  \_\_\_\_\_\_ a good cook; she made delicious fried chicken.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This pronoun refers to "Genet" so we should use the *singular* pronoun **herself**.

items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

>I asked Aaliyah and Diane if  \_\_\_\_\_\_ had recommendations for things to do in Boise, Idaho.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence refers to two people, so we should use the *plural* pronoun **they**.

items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

> I took my  \_\_\_\_\_\_ to the library to donate it.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence uses the singular pronoun "it", so we need a singular noun phrase, like **"box of books"**.

items.question_content:

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence uses the plural pronoun "they", so we need a plural noun phrase, like **"Muna and Jasmin".**

hints:

This sentence uses the singular pronoun "it", so we need a singular noun phrase, like **"list of instructions"**.

hints:

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

This sentence uses the *plural* possessive adjective "ours", so we need a noun phrase that is plural and in the first person, like **"Ahmed, Zahida, and I".**

hints:

This sentence uses the plural pronoun "they", so we need a plural noun phrase, like **"the girls"**.

items.question_content:
**Identify the choice that results in pronoun number agreement!**

> \_\_\_\_\_\_ burrowed its way through the holes in the ground.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Pronouns must agree in number with the noun they refer. So, if a noun is plural, its pronoun must also be plural:

* The **elephant** was grey → **it** was grey
* The **elephants** were grey → **they** were grey
This sentence uses the singular pronoun "it", so we need a singular noun phrase, like **"the gopher"**.
